zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice King於大約1533年出生在英國巴金哈姆爾阿斯頓 克林頓時，她的父親Kinge25歲，母親Mrs Kinge23歲。她於1553年1月16日與Henry Baldwin在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾阿斯頓 克林頓結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1626年11月23日在她的家鄉享年94歲，埋葬在英國巴金哈姆爾艾勒斯波利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alice King于大约1533年出生在英国巴金哈姆爾阿斯頓 克林頓时，她的父亲Kinge25岁，母亲Mrs Kinge23岁。她于1553年1月16日与Henry Baldwin在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾阿斯頓 克林頓结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1626年11月23日在她的家乡享年94岁，埋葬在英国巴金哈姆爾艾勒斯波利。

nameMeanings.name: King
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English king ‘king’ (Old English cyning, cyng), perhaps acquired by someone with kingly qualities or as a pageant name by someone who had acted the part of a king or had been chosen as the master of ceremonies or ‘king’ of an event such as a tournament, festival or folk ritual. In North America, the surname King has absorbed several European cognates and equivalents with the same meaning, for example German König (see Koenig ) and Küng, French Roy , Slovenian, Croatian, or Serbian Kralj , Polish Krol . It is also very common among African Americans. It is also found as an artificial Jewish surname.
English: occasionally from the Middle English personal name King, originally an Old English nickname from the vocabulary word cyning, cyng ‘king’.
Irish: adopted for a variety of names containing the syllable rí (which means ‘king’ in Irish).

variants.name: Kingdon
meanings: English (southwestern, especially Devon): habitational name from Higher Kingdon in Alverdiscott, or from Kendon in North Bovey, both in Devon. Both are named in Old English from cyning ‘king’ (see King ) or cyne- ‘royal’ + dūn ‘hill’.

variants.name: Chief
meanings: Native American: translation into English (and shortening) of a personal name based on a word, such as Cheyenne veho, meaning ‘chief’. See also King 4.

variants.name: Koenig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Erwin, Dieter, Frieda, Monika, Reinhard, Egon, Fritz, Heinz, Wilhelm.

German (mainly König) and Jewish (Ashkenazic; also König): from Middle High German künic, German König ‘king’, hence a German nickname for a servant or retainer of a king (for example, a farmer on a royal demesne), or alternatively a status name for the head of a craftmen's guild, or a society of sharpshooters or minstrels. As a Jewish surname, it is artificial, one of several such Ashkenazic names based on European titles of nobility or royalty. The surname Koenig is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Poland. In eastern Germany the surname König is in part a Germanized form (translation into German) of Sorbian Kral ‘king’. Similarly, in Czechia, Poland, Slovenia, and Croatia, it was often applied as a translation into German of Czech, Polish, Slovenian, and Croatian cognates Král (see Kral ), Król (see Krol ), and Kralj , respectively. Compare Konig .

variants.name: Kings
meanings: English (Worcestershire): variant of King , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 434, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Alice King was born about 1533, in Aston Clinton, Buckinghamshire, England, her father, Kinge, was 25 and her mother, Mrs Kinge, was 23. She married Henry Baldwin on 16 January 1553, in Aston Clinton, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 23 November 1626, in her hometown, at the age of 94, and was buried in Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alice King est née environ 1533, à Aston Clinton, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, Kinge, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Mrs Kinge, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Henry Baldwin le 16 janvier 1553, à Aston Clinton, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 23 novembre 1626, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 94 ans, et a été enterrée à Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre.

Lei sposato Henry Baldwin il 16 gennaio 1553, nel Aston Clinton, Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Alice Kingが約1533年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアアストン クリントンで生まれた時，父Kingeは25歳，母Mrs Kingeは23歳であった。1553年1月16日にイギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアアストン クリントンでHenry Baldwinと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1626年11月23日に故郷で94歳で死去し，イギリスブクインガムシュアイアエイレスブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice King(이)가 대략 1533년 영국 벅잉함실르 애스턴 클린턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Kinge(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs Kinge(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1553년 1월 16일 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 애스턴 클린턴에서 Henry Baldwin(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1626년 11월 23일 그녀의 고향에서 94세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 벅잉함실르 아일레스버이에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Henry Baldwin em 16 de janeiro de 1553, em Aston Clinton, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alice King родилась около 1533 г. в местности астон клинтон, буккингхамшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Kinge было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs Kinge, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry Baldwin 16 января 1553 г. в местности астон клинтон, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла 23 ноября 1626 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 94 лет и была похоронена в местности аилесбури, буккингхамшир, Англия.
